name: Les Amoureuses
description: Most famous Premier Cru in Burgundy, often considered Grand Cru quality. Supremely elegant and perfumed.
historicalNotes: Name means 'The Lovers.' Borders Musigny Grand Cru and shares many characteristics.

name: Les Charmes
description: Charming, accessible wines with typical Chambolle elegance.
historicalNotes: One of the largest Premier Crus in Chambolle-Musigny.

name: Les Fuées
description: Located between Musigny and Bonnes-Mares. Structured yet elegant.
historicalNotes: Jacques-Frédéric Mugnier makes benchmark examples.

name: Clos Saint-Jacques
description: Widely considered Grand Cru quality. Powerful, structured, age-worthy. The best Premier Cru in Gevrey.
historicalNotes: Shared by five top producers including Rousseau, Fourrier, and Jadot. Should be Grand Cru.

name: Les Cazetiers
description: Structured, mineral-driven wines. One of Gevrey's top Premier Crus.
historicalNotes: Located just below Clos Saint-Jacques. Armand Rousseau makes stellar examples.

name: Lavaux Saint-Jacques
description: Elegant, perfumed style unusual for Gevrey. Excellent aging potential.
historicalNotes: Name from Chapel of St Jacques that once stood here.

name: Clos de la Perrière
description: Top Premier Cru of Fixin. Rocky, well-drained soil produces structured wines.
historicalNotes: Monopole of Domaine de la Perrière (formerly Domaine Joliet).

name: Les Saint-Georges
description: Village namesake and top Premier Cru. Grand Cru quality. Powerful, structured, long-lived.
historicalNotes: Should arguably be Grand Cru. Village took its name from this climat.

name: Les Vaucrains
description: Most powerful Premier Cru in Nuits. Dark, structured, mineral-driven.
historicalNotes: Henri Gouges and Robert Chevillon make benchmark examples.

name: Les Cailles
description: More elegant than many Nuits Premier Crus. Refined, complex.
historicalNotes: Name means 'quails.' Robert Chevillon monopole within the climat.

name: Les Suchots
description: Located just below Richebourg Grand Cru. Rich, structured wines approaching Grand Cru quality.
historicalNotes: Large Premier Cru with many excellent producers.

name: Les Beaux Monts
description: Elegant, perfumed Premier Cru on slopes above village. Beautiful aromatics.
historicalNotes: Name means 'beautiful hills.' Includes several subdivisions.

name: Aux Malconsorts
description: Adjacent to La Tâche. Rich, powerful wines often of Grand Cru quality.
historicalNotes: Domaine de la Romanée-Conti owns parcel here (Clos des Malconsorts).

name: Les Perrières
description: Most prestigious Premier Cru in Meursault, widely considered Grand Cru quality. Mineral, precise, age-worthy.
historicalNotes: Name from 'perrières' (quarries). Should arguably be promoted to Grand Cru.

name: Les Genevrières
description: Rich, opulent Premier Cru with great depth. Classic Meursault style.
historicalNotes: Largest of Meursault's top Premier Crus. Name from juniper bushes (genévriers).

name: Les Charmes
description: Large Premier Cru producing charming, accessible wines with good aging potential.
historicalNotes: Includes several subdivisions. Variable quality due to size and multiple owners.

name: Les Pucelles
description: Adjacent to Montrachet and Bienvenues-Bâtard. Elegant, refined, often approaching Grand Cru quality.
historicalNotes: Name means 'maidens.' Domaine Leflaive makes benchmark examples.

name: Le Cailleret
description: High elevation Premier Cru above Montrachet. Taut, mineral, elegant.
historicalNotes: Shares slope with Chevalier-Montrachet. Similar stony terroir.

name: Les Folatières
description: Large, well-regarded Premier Cru. Rich yet refined.
historicalNotes: Borders Bâtard-Montrachet and shares similar terroir in places.

name: Les Combettes
description: On border with Meursault. Combines Puligny elegance with Meursault richness.
historicalNotes: Étienne Sauzet and Leflaive make outstanding examples.

name: Les Ruchottes
description: Top white Premier Cru in Chassagne. Mineral, precise, age-worthy.
historicalNotes: Name from 'roches' (rocks). Rocky terroir similar to Chevalier-Montrachet.

name: Les Caillerets
description: Excellent white Premier Cru producing taut, mineral wines.
historicalNotes: Abuts Montrachet and Bâtard-Montrachet Grand Crus.

name: Les Vergers
description: Well-regarded Premier Cru for both whites and reds. Balanced, elegant.
historicalNotes: Name means 'orchards.' Planted to both Chardonnay and Pinot Noir.

name: Clos de la Boudriotte
description: Small walled vineyard producing excellent reds and whites.
historicalNotes: Ramonet makes stellar examples of both colors.

name: Les Clos des Mouches
description: Most prestigious Premier Cru in Beaune. Elegant wines, both red and white.
historicalNotes: Name means 'enclosure of flies' or possibly 'bees.' Drouhin owns large portion.

name: Les Grèves
description: Gravelly soil produces structured, age-worthy reds. One of Beaune's finest.
historicalNotes: Name from 'grèves' (gravel). Contains famous Vigne de l'Enfant Jésus monopole.

name: Les Bressandes
description: Well-structured Premier Cru with excellent aging potential.
historicalNotes: Located in northern sector of Beaune Premier Crus.

name: Les Rugiens
description: Most famous Premier Cru in Pommard, often considered Grand Cru quality. Powerful, structured, long-lived.
historicalNotes: Name from 'rouge' (red) referring to iron-rich soil. Divided into Rugiens Bas and Rugiens Hauts.

name: Les Épenots
description: Second-best Premier Cru in Pommard. Rich, structured wines.
historicalNotes: Includes Clos des Épeneaux monopole (Comte Armand).

name: Les Caillerets
description: Top Premier Cru in Volnay. Elegant, perfumed wines with great finesse.
historicalNotes: High elevation produces particularly refined wines. Contains Clos des 60 Ouvrées monopole.

name: Les Santenots
description: Technically in Meursault but red wines can be labeled Volnay. Rich, powerful style.
historicalNotes: Unique situation where reds from Meursault take Volnay appellation.

name: Clos des Ducs
description: Monopole of Marquis d'Angerville. Benchmark Volnay combining elegance and structure.
historicalNotes: One of the most famous monopoles in Burgundy.
